risk_name: Iridium Supply Constraint and Price Volatility for PEM Manufacturing

description.summary: Iridium is a critical material for PEM electrolysis (~0.3-0.5 g/kW loading). Current global production (~8 tonnes/year, >80% from South Africa) supports ~16-25 GW/year PEM manufacturing. At current loading, a 50 GW/year PEM market would require 15-25 tonnes/year of iridium — exceeding current production. Price volatility and supply concentration create CAPEX uncertainty for PEM projects.
description.detailed: Iridium is one of the rarest elements on Earth (~0.001 ppm crustal abundance). It is produced as a byproduct of platinum mining, primarily in South Africa (>80% of global production). Iridium price has been highly volatile: ~€30,000/kg (2016) → €150,000/kg (2021) → €120,000/kg (2025). At 0.3-0.5 g/kW loading, iridium contributes €45-75/kW to PEM stack cost at current prices. Technology roadmap: loading reduction to <0.1 g/kW through advanced supported catalysts and core-shell nanoparticles is expected by 2030 but not yet commercialized. Iridium recycling from end-of-life stacks is currently 0% — first commercial recycling expected 2028-2030 when first large-scale PEM plants reach stack replacement.
description.root_cause: Iridium is a byproduct of platinum mining with inelastic supply. South Africa (>80% of production) creates geopolitical concentration risk. PEM electrolysis competes with electronics and chemical industries for iridium supply.
trigger_events: Iridium spot price exceeds €200,000/kg for >30 consecutive days
Major South African PGM mine announces production cut >10%
Iridium loading reduction technology fails to meet commercial readiness milestones
Competing demand (electronics, chemical catalysts) increases significantly

assessment.probability_rationale: Current supply is adequate for announced PEM project pipeline through 2028-2030. Iridium loading reduction technology is progressing well. Supply constraint is a medium-term (2030+) risk, not an immediate threat.
assessment.impact_rationale: If iridium supply constrains PEM manufacturing, stack costs would increase 30-50% (€240-400/kW additional at current loading). Projects without fixed-price OEM contracts would face significant CAPEX overrun.
assessment.detectability_rationale: Iridium prices are publicly quoted daily. PGM mine production is reported quarterly. Technology roadmaps are public. Perfect market visibility.
assessment.assessment_method: Technology Card evidence + PGM market analysis + academic literature on iridium loading reduction

cost.description: At 200 MW PEM plant: €30M additional stack cost if iridium price doubles and loading is not yet reduced. Mitigated by fixed-price OEM contracts.

preventive_actions.description: Negotiate fixed-price stack supply agreement with iridium price escalation capped at agreed index. Transfer iridium price risk to OEM where possible.
Specify iridium loading <0.3 g/kW in stack technical specification to future-proof against supply constraints

incident_descriptions: Normand'Hy (GA-PR-001): 200 MW PEM, 12 modules. Estimated iridium requirement: ~80-120 kg at current loading. Siemens Energy has direct iridium sourcing agreements with South African PGM miners — vertical integration mitigates this risk at the OEM level.
evidence.lessons_learned: Fixed-price OEM contracts with agreed iridium price indices transfer supply risk. Iridium loading reduction is the single most important PEM technology development for GW-scale viability.

sources.title: Iridium supply and demand for PEM electrolysis: 2025-2050 projections — J. Cleaner Production, 2025
sources.author: Journal of Cleaner Production
